sections.label: Section 3.380
sections.translation: a dark mist over his house .

sections.label: Section 4.381
sections.translation: For it remains . We are skilled in plotting , powerful in execution , and we remember evil deeds ; we are revered and hard for mortals to appease ,

sections.label: Section 4.385
sections.translation: pursuing our allotted office which is without rights , without honor , separated from the gods in sunless light — our office that makes the path rough for seeing and dim-sighted alike .

sections.label: Section 4.389
sections.translation: What mortal , then , does not

sections.label: Section 4.390
sections.translation: stand in awe and dread of this , when he hears from me the law ordained by Fate , given by the gods for perfect fulfilment ? My ancient privilege still remains , I do not meet with dishonor ,

sections.label: Section 4.395
sections.translation: although I have my place under the earth and in sunless darkness .

sections.label: Section 397
sections.translation: From afar I heard the call of a summons , from the Scamander , while I was taking possession of the land , which the leaders and chiefs of the Achaeans

sections.label: Section 400
sections.translation: assigned to me , a great portion of the spoil their spears had won , to be wholly mine forever , a choice gift to Theseus ’ sons . [*] From there I have come , urging on my tireless foot , without wings rustling the folds of my aegis ,

sections.label: Section 405
sections.translation: yoking this chariot to colts in their prime . [*] As I see this strange company of visitors to my land , I am not afraid , but it is a wonder to my eyes . Who in the world are you ? I address you all in common — this stranger sitting at my image ,

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: ὑμᾶς θ ὁμοίας οὐδενὶ σπαρτῶν γένει ,
sections.translation: and you , who are like no race of creatures ever born , neither seen by gods among goddesses nor resembling mortal forms . But it is far from just to speak ill of one ’ s neighbor who is blameless , and Right stands aloof .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: πεύσει τὰ πάντα συντόμως , Διὸς κόρη .
sections.translation: Daughter of Zeus , you will hear it all in brief . We are the eternal children of Night . We are called Curses in our homes beneath the earth .

sections.label: Section 418
sections.original: γένος μὲν οἶδα κληδόνας τ ἐπωνύμους .
sections.translation: I now know your family and the names by which you are called .

sections.label: Section 419
sections.original: τιμάς γε μὲν δὴ τὰς ἐμὰς πεύσει τάχα .
sections.translation: You will soon learn my office .

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: μάθοιμ ἄν , εἰ λέγοι τις ἐμφανῆ λόγον .
sections.translation: I shall understand , if someone would tell the story clearly .

sections.label: Section 421
sections.translation: We drive murderers from their homes .

sections.label: Section 422
sections.original: καὶ τῷ κτανόντι ποῦ τὸ [*] τέρμα τῆς φυγῆς ; [*]
sections.translation: And where is the end of flight for the killer ?

sections.label: Section 423
sections.translation: Where joy is absent and unknown . [*]

sections.label: Section 424
sections.original: ἦ καὶ τοιαύτας τῷδ ἐπιρροιζεῖς [*] φυγάς ;
sections.translation: And would you drive this man with your shrieks to such flight ?

sections.label: Section 425
sections.translation: Yes , for he thought it right to be his mother ’ s murderer .

sections.label: Section 426
sections.original: ἄλλαις ἀνάγκαις , [*] ἤ τινος [*] τρέων κότον ;
sections.translation: Through other compulsions , or in fear of someone ’ s wrath ?

sections.label: Section 427
sections.original: ποῦ γὰρ τοσοῦτο κέντρον ὡς μητροκτονεῖν ;
sections.translation: Where is there a spur so keen as to compel the murder of a mother ?

sections.label: Section 428
sections.translation: Two parties are present ; only half the case is heard .

sections.label: Section 429
sections.original: ἀλλ ὅρκον οὐ δέξαιτ ἄν , οὐ δοῦναι θέλοι . [*]
sections.translation: But he will not receive an oath nor does he want to give one .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: κλύειν δίκαιος [*] μᾶλλον ἢ πρᾶξαι θέλεις .
sections.translation: You want to be called just rather than to act justly .

sections.label: Section 431
sections.original: πῶς δή ; δίδαξον : τῶν σοφῶν γὰρ οὐ πένει .
sections.translation: How so ? Teach me . For you are not poor in subtleties .

sections.label: Section 432
sections.original: ὅρκοις τὰ μὴ δίκαια μὴ νικᾶν λέγω .
sections.translation: I say that oaths must not win victory for injustice .

sections.label: Section 433
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐξέλεγχε , κρῖνε δ εὐθεῖαν δίκην .
sections.translation: Well then , question him , and make a straight judgment .

sections.label: Section 434
sections.original: ἦ κἀπ ἐμοὶ τρέποιτ ἂν αἰτίας τέλος ;
sections.translation: Then would you turn over the decision of the charge to me ?

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: πῶς δ οὔ ; σέβουσαί γ ἀξίαν κἀπ ἀξίων . [*]
sections.translation: How not ?— since we honor you because you are worthy and of worthy parentage .

sections.label: Section 436
sections.original: τί πρὸς τάδ εἰπεῖν , ὦ ξέν , ἐν μέρει θέλεις ;
sections.translation: What do you want to say to this , stranger , in turn ? After you name your country and family and fortunes , then defend yourself against this charge ; if indeed , relying on the justice of your case ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.translation: you sit clinging to my image near my hearth , as a sacred suppliant , like Ixion . [*] To all this give me a plain answer .

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: ἄνασσ Ἀθάνα , πρῶτον ἐκ τῶν ὑστάτων
sections.translation: Lady Athena , first of all I will take away a great anxiety from your last words .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: οὐκ εἰμὶ προστρόπαιος , οὐδ ἔχων [*] μύσος
sections.translation: I am not a suppliant in need of purification , nor did I sit at your image with pollution on my hands . I will give you strong proof of this . It is the law for one who is defiled by shedding blood to be barred from speech until he is sprinkled with the blood of

sections.label: Section 450
sections.translation:
a new-born victim by a man who can purify from murder . Long before at other houses I have been thus purified both by victims and by flowing streams .
And so I declare that this concern is out of the way . As to my family , you will soon learn .

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: Ἀργεῖός εἰμι , πατέρα δ ἱστορεῖς καλῶς ,
sections.translation: I am an Argive ; my father — you rightly inquire about him — was Agamemnon , the commander of the naval forces ; along with him , you made Troy , the city of Ilion , to be no city . He did not die nobly , after he came home ; but my black-hearted

sections.label: Section 460
sections.translation: mother killed him after she covered him in a crafty snare that still remains to witness his murder in the bath . And when I came back home , having been an exile in the time before , I killed the woman who gave birth to me , I will not deny it , as the penalty in return for the murder of my dearly-loved father .

sections.label: Section 465
sections.translation: Together with me Loxias is responsible for this deed , because he threatened me with pains , a goad for my heart , if I should fail to do this deed to those who were responsible . You judge whether I acted justly or not ; whatever happens to me at your hands , I will be content .

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: τὸ πρᾶγμα μεῖζον , εἴ τις οἴεται τόδε
sections.translation: The matter is too great , if any mortal thinks to pass judgment on it ; no , it is not lawful even for me to decide on cases of murder that is followed by the quick anger of the Furies , especially since you , by rites fully performed , have come a pure and harmless suppliant to my house ;

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: ἐγὼ [*] δ ἄμομφον ὄντα σ αἱροῦμαι [*] πόλει .
sections.translation: and so I respect you , since you do not bring harm to my city . Yet these women have an office that does not permit them to be dismissed lightly ; and if they fail to win their cause , the venom from their resentment will fall upon the ground , an intolerable , perpetual plague afterwards in the land .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: τοιαῦτα μὲν τάδ ἐστίν : ἀμφότερα , μένειν
sections.translation: So stands the case : either course — to let them stay , to drive them out — brings disaster and perplexity to me . But since this matter has fallen here , I will select judges of homicide bound by oath , and I will establish this tribunal for all time .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: ὑμεῖς δὲ μαρτύριά τε καὶ τεκμήρια
sections.translation: Summon your witnesses and proofs , sworn evidence to support your case ; and I will return when I have chosen the best of my citizens , for them to decide this matter truly , after they take an oath that they will pronounce no judgment contrary to justice . Exit

sections.label: Section 1.490
sections.translation: Here is the overturning of new laws , if the wrongful cause of this matricide is to triumph . Now his deed will accustom all men

sections.label: Section 1.495
sections.translation: to recklessness ; many sorrowful wounds , given in truth by children , wait for parents in the future time .

sections.label: Section 1.499
sections.translation: For the wrath of us , the Furies who keep watch on mortals ,

sections.label: Section 1.500
sections.translation: will not come stealthily upon such deeds — I will let loose death in every form . And as he anticipates his neighbor ’ s evils , one man

sections.label: Section 1.505
sections.translation: will ask of another when hardship is to end or to decrease ; and the poor wretch offers the vain consolation of uncertain remedies .

sections.label: Section 2.508
sections.translation: Do not let anyone who is struck by misfortune make an appeal

sections.label: Section 2.510
sections.translation: and cry aloud this word , Justice ! Thrones of the Furies ! Perhaps some father , or mother , in new sorrow ,

sections.label: Section 2.515
sections.translation: may cry out these words piteously , now that the house of Justice is falling .

sections.label: Section 2.517
sections.translation: There is a time when fear is good and ought to remain seated as a guardian of the heart .

sections.label: Section 2.520
sections.translation: It is profitable to learn wisdom under strain . But who , if he did not train his heart in fear , either city or mortal ,

sections.label: Section 2.525
sections.translation: would still revere justice in the same way ?

sections.label: Section 3.526
sections.translation: Do not approve of a lawless life or one subject to a tyrant .

sections.label: Section 3.530
sections.translation: The god grants power to moderation in every form , but he oversees other matters in different ways . I have a timely word of advice : arrogance is truly the child of impiety ,

sections.label: Section 3.535
sections.translation: but from health of soul comes happiness , dear to all , much prayed for .

sections.label: Section 3.538
sections.original: ἐς τὸ πᾶν δέ σοι λέγω ,
sections.translation: And as for the whole matter , I say to you : respect the altar of Justice

sections.label: Section 3.540
sections.translation: and do not , looking to profit , dishonor it by spurning with godless foot ; for punishment will come upon you . The appointed fulfilment remains .

sections.label: Section 3.545
sections.translation: Therefore , let a man rightly put first in honor the reverence owed to his parents , and have regard for attentions paid to guests welcomed in his house .

sections.label: Section 4.550
sections.original: ἑκὼν δ [*] ἀνάγκας ἄτερ δίκαιος ὢν
sections.translation: Whoever is just willingly and without compulsion will not lack happiness ; he will never be utterly destroyed . But I say that the man who boldly transgresses , amassing a great heap unjustly — by force ,

sections.label: Section 4.555
sections.translation: in time , he will strike his sail , when trouble seizes him as the yardarm is splintered .
